claim: A crowd of well-meaning people at the first sighting of a panicked, fearful dog caused her to bolt 7 miles in a single hour from her escape point — a quantified displacement event caused by human interference.
stance_target: crowd-at-sighting as a measurable displacement trigger for fearful lost dogs
scope_conditions: Xenophobic/trauma-history dog (puppy mill background); single observed incident
quoted_support: The well-meaning crowd of people trying to 'help' Winnie actually triggered her flight instinct. In a single hour, Winnie bolted and ran 7 miles from her escape point to Leisure World.
numbers: from escape point to Leisure World

claim: After initial panic flight, a fearful lost dog settled into a stable territory on a quiet farm property for weeks/months — demonstrating a transition from active flight phase to territorial resting phase.
stance_target: temporal phase transition in lost dogs — flight phase to settled/territorial phase
scope_conditions: Trauma-history dog; territory settlement occurred after displacement from initial sighting area; required quiet, human-sparse environment
quoted_support: Eventually, she settled on a beautiful property bordered by quiet farms. It was a sanctuary, of sorts, for a dog who feared four walls.

claim: A dog with prior confinement trauma (puppy mill background) refused to enter humane traps for months due to conditioned fear of enclosures — temperament/trauma history modulates trap susceptibility.
stance_target: prior trauma history as a modifier of trap-based recovery protocol effectiveness
scope_conditions: Puppy mill background = confinement trauma; applies to any dog with prior negative confinement experience
quoted_support: Winnie continued to refuse to enter our enclosure trap, known as 'The Missy Trap.' This was likely due to her past trauma with confinement.

claim: A familiar rescue dog from the lost dog's original rescue organization served as the social bridge that enabled capture when all traps and other dogs had failed — 'social lure' via conspecific with shared history.
stance_target: conspecific social lure as a recovery method for trap-shy, fear-aggressive lost dogs
scope_conditions: Trap-shy, trauma-history dog; specific familiar conspecific (same rescue) required — other dogs failed
quoted_support: Where other dogs had failed to spark a connection with Winnie, Phoebe was the key! The dog who had outmaneuvered every trap we had set and feared every technique we had tried began to follow Phoebe with pure, wagging excitement.
entities: A Home of Their Own Rescue

claim: Thermal drones provide continuous nighttime surveillance of a skittish lost dog's movement patterns — 16 hours of continuous drone monitoring used in this case to track territorial behavior.
stance_target: thermal drone as a non-intrusive surveillance method for tracking settled fearful lost dogs
scope_conditions: Night surveillance; thermal lens required for detection; Arkcan Drone Services cited
quoted_support: We brought in Ryan from Arkcan Drone Services, watching Winnie through a thermal lens for 16 hours straight. We watched her fiercely patrol her food, chasing off foxes to claim her territory.
